qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OAuth2 authentication bypass via configuration flag controlled by external NRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets NRF-controlled OAuth2Required authentication bypass logic, not the Nudm_SDM PATCH empty-supi forwarding/error handling root cause described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-side request forgery via unvalidated callback URLs in registration data
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unvalidated callback URLs used for outbound notifications; it does not identify the advisory's empty supi path parameter, PATCH-to-PUT downstream forwarding, or 400-to-500 error translation.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded HTTP request body consumption enables memory exhaustion DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes generic unbounded request body reads and missing server timeouts, not the advisory's malformed empty-supi PATCH request handling and downstream error mapping flaw.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Weak PRNG usage in authentication enables predictable authentication vectors
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is about timestamp-seeded math/rand use in authentication vector generation; it is unrelated to the advisory's Nudm_SDM PATCH sdm-subscriptions empty-supi error handling behavior.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: free5GC UDM Nudm_SDM PATCH handling for sdm-subscriptions with an empty supi path segment, incorrect downstream PATCH-to-PUT forwarding to UDR, and propagation of a downstream 400 Bad Request as a 500 SYSTEM_FAILURE.
evidence: The advisory describes an Improper Error Handling issue in UDM PATCH sdm-subscriptions requests where an empty supi path parameter is mishandled and a downstream UDR 400 is returned to the client as a UDM 500.
The qualifying reports cover import deserialization RCE, NRF-controlled OAuth2 bypass, callback URL SSRF, generic unbounded request body consumption, and weak randomness in authentication generation.
None of the qualifying reports identifies the empty supi path parameter, PATCH-to-PUT translation, UDR 400-to-UDM 500 mapping, or equivalent Nudm_SDM source-to-sink/control-flow condition.
